questions.question: Consider the non-convex optimization problem: minimize f(x) = x^2 + 5sin(x) subject to a constraint g(x) = x - 2 = 0. Determine the conditions under which this problem converges to a critical point. (5 marks)
A non-convex function f(x) is defined as f(x) = x^4 - 2x^2 + 1. Sketch the level curves of this function and identify the critical points. (5 marks)

An optimization problem is defined as minimize f(x) = x^3 - 3x subject to a constraint g(x) = x^2 - 4 = 0. Find the critical points of this problem by setting the gradient of the objective function equal to zero. (5 marks)
